items.headline: Edwards, Timberwolves overcome big game by Wembanyama to beat Spurs
items.blurb: Edwards, Timberwolves overcome big game by Wembanyama to beat Spurs — Anthony Edwards scored 18 points in his unexpected return from injury and the Minnesota Timberwolves overcame a huge game by Victor Wembanyama and held on to beat the San Antonio Spurs 104-102 in the opener of the Western Conference semifinals on Monday night.

items.blurb: Spurs’ Champagnie just misses game-winning buzzer beater after wild sequence — Watch as San Antonio Spurs forward Julian Champagnie just misses a game-winning buzzer beater after a wild end-of-game sequence in Game 1 against the Minnesota Timberwolves.

items.blurb: Spurs’ Wembanyama sets playoff record with 11th block vs. Timberwolves — Watch as Victor Wembanyama records his 11th block of the game, setting a new playoff record, before it sparks a fast-break bucket for the San Antonio Spurs against the Minnesota Timberwolves.

items.headline: Spurs’ Wembanyama breaks record for blocks in playoff game
items.blurb: Spurs’ Wembanyama breaks record for blocks in playoff game — In just his first playoff run, Victor Wembanyama has already entered his name into the record books.

items.headline: Jalen Brunson makes unique history in Knicks' Game 1 win over 76ers
items.blurb: Jalen Brunson makes unique history in Knicks' Game 1 win over 76ers — Jalen Brunson and the New York Knicks were absolutely pumping iron on Monday to begin their series against the Philadelphia 76ers. It was a blowout win for the Knicks, with the team towering over the 76ers, 137-98.

items.headline: Timberwolves’ Edwards splashes triple over Wembanyama in isolation
items.blurb: Timberwolves’ Edwards splashes triple over Wembanyama in isolation — Watch as Minnesota Timberwolves star guard Anthony Edwards swishes a deep three over the outstretched arm of Victor Wembanyama in isolation.

items.headline: Knicks stay red-hot, cruise past 76ers in Game 1
items.blurb: Knicks stay red-hot, cruise past 76ers in Game 1 — Jalen Brunson scored 27 of his 35 points in the first half and the New York Knicks emphatically added to a historic postseason roll by overwhelming the Philadelphia 76ers 137-98 on Monday night in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference semifinals.

items.headline: Jalen Brunson, Knicks make history with third-straight blowout win
items.blurb: Jalen Brunson, Knicks make history with third-straight blowout win — With their series-opening win over the Philadelphia 76ers, the New York Knicks sent a message and continued to look unbeatable. The Knicks grabbed a 1-0 lead in their second-round series, dominating the 76ers to the tune of 137-98.

items.headline: Timberwolves’ Edwards talks trash to Spurs bench after drilling three
items.blurb: Timberwolves’ Edwards talks trash to Spurs bench after drilling three — Watch as Minnesota Timberwolves star guard Anthony Edwards talks some trash to the San Antonio Spurs bench after hitting a pull-up three, saying “I’m back” in his return from a knee injury.

items.headline: Three things Lakers need to do vs. Thunder
items.blurb: Three things Lakers need to do vs. Thunder — OKC has been the best offensive team thus far in the playoffs, and for LeBron James and his crew to stand a chance, they'll have to find multiple ways to slow down this well-oiled machine. Here's how they can manage this.

items.headline: Raptors face several challenges in bid to maintain momentum in off-season

items.blurb: Bryson Graham Hired By Bulls As Executive Vice President Of Basketball Operations — Bryson Graham has been hired by the Chicago Bulls as Executive Vice President of Basketball Operations. items.blurb: J.B. Bickerstaff, Pistons Sign Contract Extension — J.B. Bickerstaff and the Detroit Pistons have signed a contract extension. The announcement comes less than 24 hours after the Pistons advanced out of the first round with their Game 7 win over the Orlando Magic. items.headline: Notebook: Raptors reflect on season, series vs. Cavaliers
items.blurb: Notebook: Raptors reflect on season, series vs. Cavaliers — “It’s only the beginning.” Heading into the season, making the playoffs looked to be the goal. But after pushing the Cavs to the limit, the Raptors’ goal posts shifted. They’ll carry that new weight of expectation in next season, Kai Gammage writes.

items.blurb: ‘We will win in Dallas’: Mavericks hire Masai Ujiri as team president — The former Toronto Raptors president has been hired as the Dallas Mavericks’ new team president and alternate governor, replacing Nico Harrison, who was fired in November, ESPN’s Shams Charania and Tim MacMahon reported Monday.
